creator: swell.k@gmail.com
text:
Cy Schubert <Cy.Schubert@komquats.com> writes:

> You don't need the configure patch. Configure.in is all you need, then run
> autoconf, e.g. USE_AUTOTOOLS=autoconf:2.62. Huge configure script patches
> are unmaintainable. I'll need to rework patch-ad into it though. I may not
> have time for this until the weekend. My evening is spoken for tonight.

That extra patch includes diff against configure.in, as well. I thought
converting the port to use autotools was beyond the scope of this PR.

>
> I've maintained large patches on IBM mainframes in a past life. Maintaining
> large patches is not a good practice and probably better to have the
> feature added at the source (the largest source code patch I maintained was
> a 10,000 line patch to IBM's JES/2 to add functionality to support Remote
> Advanced Function Printers). That's my take on large patches.
